$comment: Harness scope surface (note 9). The foundational data structure: scheduler picks the in_progress item, verifier gates active->passing, handoff reads state. JSON (not MD) so it isn't casually overwritten. Every feature starts failing; it must EARN 'passing' via its verification command + recorded evidence (commit hash). Hooks: check-wip.py (exactly one in_progress), check-evidence.py (no 'passing' without evidence).
rules: One feature in_progress at a time. active->passing ONLY when `verification` runs green AND `evidence` records the proof (commit hash + verify reference). Do not hand-edit a feature to 'passing'.

features.behavior: Reconcile stale feature_list.json statuses + refresh PROGRESS.md: confirm R2.2x tests pass, fix -k selectors to match real test names, update PROGRESS.md to reflect true HEAD (core/infra done; gateway done; worker idle skeleton; next = Phase 1 domain cards). Fix Ryuk testcontainers issue so integration tests run cleanly.
verification: python .claude/scripts/check-wip.py && python .claude/scripts/check-evidence.py (exit 0)
uv run pytest tests/unit -k 'schemas or error_handlers' (10 passed)
uv run pytest tests/integration -k ingestion (7 passed)
features.evidence: 49 unit + 7 integration passed (make check green); Ryuk fix in conftest.py; tracker reconciled; commit ed6693e HEAD

features.behavior: Structured logging (core/infra/logging.py): configure_logging() + get_logger(name); job_id/task_id bound via contextvars so concurrent jobs don't bleed ids; every log line carries job_id (the trace key). No print/breakpoint anywhere.
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k logging (bound job_id present in emitted line; two concurrent contexts don't bleed ids; pure, no Docker)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/unit -k logging -> 6 passed (configure, bind, filter stamp, get_logger, concurrent isolation). Commit ab425da + committed ac9dee0 + 61d73fe

features.behavior: Pydantic event contracts JobCreated/TtsRequested/StitchReady, each carrying event_id (pointers, never bytes).
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k events
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/unit -k events -> 7 passed; commit b6f68f3 (fmt b5a7870)

features.behavior: Vendor simulation & fault injection (the probe substrate per SPEC 1-2): simulate parse/TTS vendor calls with asyncio.sleep + seedable randomized failure. Parse injects a 15% 500-error rate (transient -> exercises retry). A deterministic 'poison' manuscript always fails (-> exercises DLQ after 3). Seedable so failure behavior is unit-assertable.
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k fault_injection (rate=0.0 never fails; rate=1.0 always fails; fixed seed reproducible; poison manuscript always raises SAME retryable VendorError; parse returns D3 blocks; tts keyed on D4 hash; pure, no Docker)
features.evidence: RECONCILED per SPEC §1 (poison = consistently-failing -> DLQ after 3 retries, single retryable VendorError; rejected card's non-retryable PoisonError -> see docs/DECISIONS.md 2026-06-25 R2.0). vendor.py re-scoped: stays in core/domain (no _sim.py), composes D3 split_blocks + D4 content_hash (removed divergent per-line splitter + inline sha256). uv run pytest tests/unit -k fault_injection -> 10 passed; full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 43 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit 0aff00f HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Broker topology declared: exchange + q.parse/q.tts/q.stitch + per-stage retry ladder (1/4/16s) + q.dlq.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k topology (RabbitMQ via testcontainers)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k topology -> 5 passed (live queues, retry queues with TTL/DLX, retry routing, DLQ after max_retries, x-retry-count header). H-XDEATH + H-TTLHOL addressed. Commit ab425da + committed ac9dee0 + 61d73fe

features.behavior: Gateway lifespan startup wiring (G2): on startup open robust broker.connect + channel + declare topology + grab the pipeline exchange; db engine; storage.ensure_bucket(); stash on app.state. Open once (not per-request); close cleanly on shutdown.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k lifespan (app starts; app.state.exchange usable to publish; shuts down without leaking connections)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k lifespan -> 1 passed (lifespan startup + GET /health 200). commit 61d73fe

features.behavior: Gateway pydantic schemas (gateway/schemas.py, G4): CreateJobRequest{manuscript, callback_url?}, JobAccepted{job_id}, JobStatusResponse{job_id, status, ...}. Empty manuscript allowed (becomes a 0-block job that still terminates).
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k schemas (valid body parses; missing manuscript -> validation error; pure, no Docker)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/unit -k schemas -> 6 passed (valid, callback, empty ok, missing->422, JobAccepted, JobStatusResponse). commit 61d73fe

features.behavior: Gateway CORS + error handlers (G3): CORSMiddleware + exception handlers. Bad body -> 422 structured JSON; unhandled raise -> 500 JSON (never an HTML stack page). Error bodies machine-readable, carrying job_id when one exists.
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k error_handlers (bad POST body -> 422 JSON; internal raise -> 500 JSON)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/unit -k error_handlers -> 4 passed (missing->422, null->422, wrong content-type->422, unhandled AttributeError->500 JSON). commit 61d73fe

features.behavior: Ingestion (G5): POST /jobs -> storage.put_text(raw/<job>.txt) -> insert Job(PENDING) + COMMIT -> THEN publish JobCreated to q.parse -> 202 + {job_id}. Dual-write order is load-bearing: MinIO -> DB commit -> publish.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k ingestion (POST /jobs -> 202 + Job PENDING in PG + raw/<job>.txt in MinIO + JobCreated on q.parse)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k ingestion -> 4 passed (202+job_id, PENDING in PG, MinIO roundtrip, JobCreated on q.parse). commit 61d73fe

features.behavior: Status read (G6): GET /status/{job_id} -> job status (+ optional pending_count progress); unknown id -> 404.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k status (known id -> its status; unknown id -> 404)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k status -> 2 passed (known->PENDING, unknown->404). commit 61d73fe

grep for x-death.count in broker.py shows 0 usages

features.behavior: Pure domain splitter (BOM 02-D3, feeds R2.3): split_blocks(manuscript) in core/domain/text.py splits a manuscript on blank-line boundaries (paragraph = block), strips whitespace, drops empties. Empty/whitespace-only -> [] (0-block path). One paragraph -> [that]. Deterministic; the fan-out width comes from here. MUST NOT raise on empty/huge input (bounding is W3/H14's job); MUST stay I/O-free (architecture test).
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k text (empty->[], whitespace->[], 1 para->1, 3 paras->3, leading/trailing blanks ignored)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/unit -k text -> 16 passed (0/1/3-block, strip, blank-line collapse, whitespace-only separator, soft-newline kept, deterministic, parametrized fan-out width); full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 41 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 68 passed; commit 0aff00f HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Pure canonical content hasher (BOM 02-D4, feeds R4.2 cache + R2.3 Task.block_hash): content_hash(text) in core/domain/hash.py returns sha256(text.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest() — a 64-char hex digest, stable across processes/runs (no salt). MUST hash the block TEXT, never the task_id (conflating the vendor-call cache key with the fan-in counter key is the named junior trap). Single canonical hasher reused by W3 (Task.block_hash) and W4 (cache + tts/<hash>.wav key). MUST stay I/O-free (architecture test).
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k hash (same text->same hash; different text->different; 64-char lowercase hex; known vectors for fixed strings)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/unit -k hash -> 12 passed (known vectors for '' and 'hello world', determinism, distinctness, 64-char lowercase hex, whitespace-significant, utf-8 non-ASCII, parametrized idempotency); full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 43 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 80 passed; commit 0aff00f HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Compare-and-set transition contract (BOM 02-D2, hardens R1.2 state.py): document in state.py that status writes MUST go through CAS (UPDATE jobs SET status=:next WHERE job_id=:id AND status IN (:expected)) not read-then-write, and that a rowcount-0 CAS is a NORMAL concurrent outcome ('someone else advanced it'), NOT an error — handlers treat it as already-advanced and proceed idempotently. Add pure helper expected_for(next) -> set[JobStatus] returning legal predecessors so handlers build the WHERE guard from one source of truth. MUST stay I/O-free (no SQLAlchemy in domain; the SQL lives in the Phase 3/4 handler).
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k 'state_machine or fsm' (existing transitions still green + expected_for predecessors correct, terminal states have no predecessors, inverse of can_transition)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/unit -k 'state_machine or fsm' -> 12 passed (8 original transition tests still green + 4 new: forward-path predecessors, FAILED=all-non-terminal, PENDING no-predecessors, exact-inverse-of-can_transition property); CAS contract + rowcount-0-is-normal documented in state.py docstring; domain stays I/O-free (test_architecture green); full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 43 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 84 passed; commit 0aff00f HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Async Redis client adapter (BOM 06-R1, root of rung R4.1): core/infra/redis.py get_redis(url) returns redis.from_url(url, decode_responses=False) (NOT awaited; keep bytes for hashes/tokens); async ping(client)->bool health helper. Uses redis.asyncio (NOT sync redis, NOT dead aioredis). Build once, share; close via aclose() (8.x rename). Mirrors infra/db.py get_engine shape.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis (ping round-trips against a real Redis container)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis -> 2 passed (ping round-trips; decode_responses=False keeps bytes) against a real redis:7-alpine testcontainer; get_redis/ping in core/infra/redis.py replace the docstring stub; full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 1 file (whole-project make check) 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit 6203aeb HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Leased N-token Redis semaphore (BOM 06-R2, Constraint A: global TTS limit=3 across ALL workers): Semaphore(slots_key, slots, lease_ttl) with acquire(owner)->token via blocking BLPOP (no busy-poll) + SET lease key NX EX, release(token) deletes lease + RPUSH token back, async-with wrapper releasing even on exception. Never asyncio.Semaphore. Cache checked BEFORE acquire (a hit burns no token).
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis (slots=2: 3rd acquire blocks until a release; never >slots tokens out)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis -> 6 passed (4 new R2: slots=2 bounds concurrency + 3rd acquire blocks then succeeds after release; acquire records tts:lease:<token> with TTL<=lease_ttl; release clears lease + returns token; async-with slot releases token+lease even on exception) against real redis:7-alpine; Semaphore (BLPOP acquire / lease SET NX EX / delete-lease-then-RPUSH release) in core/infra/redis.py; full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 44 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit 6203aeb HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Semaphore init idempotency (BOM 12-X4, the 3xN-tokens footgun): ensure_slots() seeds exactly N tokens exactly-once across ALL workers via atomic Lua (init-marker: if unset DEL slots_key + RPUSH N tokens 0..N-1 + set marker; else no-op). Converges to exactly N regardless of how many workers call it concurrently. Never unconditional RPUSH on boot.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis (5 concurrent ensure_slots -> LLEN tts:slots == N exactly)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis -> 8 passed (2 new X4: 5 workers race ensure_slots concurrently -> LLEN tts:slots == 3 exactly with token identities ['0','1','2']; ensure_slots is init-once-not-top-up: after consuming a token a 2nd ensure_slots leaves 2 not 3) against real redis:7-alpine; atomic Lua (_ENSURE_SLOTS_LUA: init-marker guard + DEL + RPUSH N + SET marker) registered in Semaphore.__init__; full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 44 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit 6203aeb HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Lease reaper: heartbeat renew + atomic reclaim (BOM 12-X5, H6): acquire spawns a heartbeat task re-SET lease XX EX every ttl/3 (cancel on release) so a healthy-but-slow holder is never reclaimed; reap() atomically (Lua, owner-checked) RPUSHes expired-but-unreturned tokens back exactly once (concurrent reapers return a token at most once). Documented as soft/best-effort limit, breaches logged.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis (stopped-heartbeat holder reclaimed after TTL; heartbeating holder keeps token; two reapers reclaim once)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis -> 11 passed (3 new X5: crashed holder's expired-lease token reclaimed by reap()->1; live heartbeating holder outlives TTL and reap()->0 leaves it; two concurrent reapers reclaim a token sum==1 with no double-return) against real redis:7-alpine; acquire spawns a ttl/3 SET-XX heartbeat (cancelled on release), reap() runs atomic owner-safe _RECLAIM_LUA per token, soft-limit breaches logged; full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 44 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit 6203aeb HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Content-hash cache (BOM 06-R3, Constraint B cost-idempotency): cache_get(h)->str|None (GET tts:cache:{h}), cache_set(h,url) (SETEX tts:cache:{h} CACHE_TTL_S url). Keyed on canonical content_hash (D4), never task_id. CACHE_TTL_S <= MinIO object lifetime (H-DANGLE). Consulted before slot acquire; not durable truth (rebuildable from MinIO).
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis (set then get returns url; missing hash -> None; entry expires after TTL)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis -> 15 passed (4 new R3: cold miss->None then set->get returns the MinIO url; miss returns None; key is tts:cache:<content_hash(D4)> with TTL 0<ttl<=3600; entry expires after TTL->None) against real redis:7-alpine; Cache class keys on D4 content_hash (never task_id), cache_set uses SET..EX (SETEX deprecated in redis-py 8); full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 44 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit 6203aeb HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Cache stampede in-flight lock (BOM backlog-H8): acquire_inflight(h)->bool true if caller won SET tts:inflight:{h} NX EX (it synthesizes); losers wait (bounded) for cache_get then use it -> at most one vendor call per identical-block burst. MUST NOT hold a TTS slot while waiting on the in-flight lock. OR a documented deliberate simplification in module + DOC2.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis (K concurrent acquire_inflight for one hash -> exactly one True; rest observe populated cache)
features.evidence: IMPLEMENTED the in-flight lock (not the simplification). uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis -> 17 passed (2 new H8: 10 concurrent acquire_inflight for one hash -> sum(won)==1 exactly one synthesiser; 5-caller stampede -> exactly one 'synth' (vendor call), the other 4 wait_for_cache and all end with the same url) against real redis:7-alpine; Cache.acquire_inflight (SET tts:inflight:<hash> NX EX) / release_inflight / wait_for_cache (bounded poll), checked WITHOUT a TTS slot to avoid pool deadlock; full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 44 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit 6203aeb HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Idempotency fast-path + inbox helpers (BOM 06-R4 / 03-B5, exactly-once effect): durable mark_event(session, event_id)->bool via dialect insert(ProcessedEvent).on_conflict_do_nothing() (True first / False duplicate) is the AUTHORITY; Redis seen_once(task_id)->bool SETNX fast-path is non-authoritative optimization (H3). NEVER inbox-skip parse (H2). processed_events retention (H10).
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k 'models or redis' (mark_event twice -> True then False; seen_once twice -> True then False; retention deletes aged rows)

features.behavior: Parse handler: split blocks, write N Task rows + pending_count=N in ONE transaction, fan-out TtsRequested xN, advance GENERATING. 0-block manuscript -> STITCHING directly (no hang).
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k parse_fanout AND -k zero_block_terminates

features.behavior: Crash recovery: manual ack LAST (after Postgres commit), prefetch set, NACK->retry path. docker kill mid-job loses no message.
verification: make e2e -k crash_recovery (docker kill worker mid-job -> redelivered, job converges)

features.behavior: Idempotent consumers: processed_events inbox (INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING) + task:done SETNX. Duplicate delivery has exactly-once effect; no double pending_count decrement.
verification: make e2e -k duplicate_delivery (same JobCreated x2 -> single effect, counter correct)

features.behavior: DLQ: poison pill dead-letters after 3 retries with exponential backoff, off the hot queue (no head-of-line blocking of healthy traffic). Job -> FAILED.
verification: make e2e -k poison_pill (-> DLQ after 3 attempts; concurrent healthy jobs still complete)

features.behavior: PENDING-sweeper/reconciler (G8, closes the gateway dual-write seam BACKLOG H1): periodic task re-publishes JobCreated for jobs stuck PENDING past a generous timeout. The Job row IS its own outbox; idempotent parse (R3.2) makes a re-publish safe. 'Ack last' is a consumer rule and cannot cover the gateway producer.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k sweeper (insert Job(PENDING) with NO event published -> after one sweep JobCreated is on q.parse and the job progresses)

features.behavior: Global TTS semaphore in Redis (leased token list, TTL): max 3 concurrent across ALL workers; a crashed worker's slot auto-reclaims via TTL.
verification: make e2e -k semaphore (>3 requested -> never >3 in flight; killed holder's slot returns)

features.behavior: TTS handler: content-hash cache check BEFORE acquiring a slot (cache hit burns no token); PUT tts/<hash>.wav; SETEX cache. Fan-in via atomic UPDATE...RETURNING -> exactly one worker sees 0 and emits StitchReady.
verification: make e2e -k cache (identical block -> no 2nd vendor call) AND -k fan_in (exactly one StitchReady)

features.behavior: Stitch & notify: concat chunks -> out/<job>.mp3 in MinIO, Job COMPLETED, fire webhook/log. Webhook failure does NOT fail the job.
verification: make e2e -k stitch (full happy path -> COMPLETED + asset) AND -k webhook_failure_still_completed

features.behavior: Observability: GET /stats exposes job/queue counts.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k stats
